brightsmith_agentAttribution.reasoning: Gold zone transformation of BLS occupation data. Reads 832 rows from base.bls_ooh (Silver), computes GRW score via piecewise linear function from employment_change_pct (8 segments mapping to 1.0-10.0 scale), computes wage_percentile_overall via PERCENT_RANK window on median_annual_wage (null-safe: excludes 23 null-wage rows from ranking then LEFT JOINs back), computes wage_percentile_education_tier via PERCENT_RANK partitioned by education_code (null-safe), derives wage_tier by bucketing wage_percentile_overall into 5 tiers (low/below_average/above_average/high/very_high), computes openings_score via PERCENT_RANK on openings_annual_avg mapped to 1-10 scale, computes market_score as weighted combination (0.6 * grw_score + 0.4 * openings_score), derives confidence_tier from wage_available + broad_occupation_flag + catchall_flag, computes data_completeness as ratio of 4 non-null core fields, sets static FutureProof stat mapping fields (backs_stats='ERN,GRW', backs_bosses='Market,Ceiling'), computes deterministic record_id via compute_grain_id with 'op' prefix, drops 6 fields (employment_change, median_wage_capped, education_typical, work_experience, training_typical, ingested_at) as redundant for Gold consumers, and promotes to consumable.occupation_profiles via idempotent promote pattern.

documentation.description: Transform Silver base.bls_ooh into the consumable.occupation_profiles Gold data product. Shapes occupation data for direct consumption by the Gemma agent and FutureProof frontend. Adds GRW score (1-10 piecewise linear from employment_change_pct), wage percentile rankings (overall and within education tier, null-safe), wage tier bucketing, market opportunity score (weighted growth + openings signal), occupation confidence tier, data completeness ratio, and static FutureProof stat/boss mappings. Drops 6 redundant Silver fields. Computes deterministic record_id with 'op' prefix. Promotes via idempotent append with dedup on record_id.

record_id.transformationDescription: Deterministic grain hash computed via compute_grain_id(row, ['soc_code'], prefix='op'). SHA-256 of concatenated grain fields with 'op' prefix. Note: prefix changes from Silver's 'ooh' to Gold's 'op', so the Gold record_id is a different hash than the Silver record_id for the same soc_code.

soc_code.transformationDescription: Direct carry from Silver. No transformation applied. XX-XXXX format validated in Silver zone.

occupation_title.transformationDescription: Direct carry from Silver. No transformation applied.

soc_major_group.transformationDescription: Direct carry from Silver. 2-digit SOC major group code was derived in Silver from soc_code[:2].

soc_major_group_name.transformationDescription: Direct carry from Silver. Human-readable major group label was derived in Silver from SOC_MAJOR_GROUP_LOOKUP.

broad_occupation_flag.transformationDescription: Direct carry from Silver. Boolean flag for 7 rolled-up broad occupation codes. Used as input to confidence_tier derivation in Gold.

catchall_flag.transformationDescription: Direct carry from Silver. Boolean flag for ~70 'all other' residual categories. Used as input to confidence_tier derivation in Gold.

employment_current.transformationDescription: Direct carry from Silver. Current employment count (actual, not thousands). Used as input to data_completeness derivation.

employment_projected.transformationDescription: Direct carry from Silver. Projected 2034 employment count.

employment_change_pct.transformationDescription: Direct carry from Silver. Percent change in employment 2024-2034. Primary input to grw_score derivation. Also used as input to data_completeness derivation.

openings_annual_avg.transformationDescription: Direct carry from Silver. Average annual job openings. Used as input to openings_score (intermediate) and market_score derivation. Also used in data_completeness derivation.

growth_category.transformationDescription: Direct carry from Silver. Bucketed growth tier (declining_fast/declining/stable/growing/growing_fast/booming) derived in Silver from employment_change_pct.

grw_score.transformationDescription: Piecewise linear function mapping employment_change_pct to a 1.0-10.0 scale via compute_grw_score(). Uses 8 segments anchored to BLS growth benchmarks: <= -20% -> 1.0 (floor), -20 to -10 -> 1.0-2.5, -10 to -1 -> 2.5-4.0, -1 to 1 -> 4.0-5.0, 1 to 5 -> 5.0-6.5, 5 to 10 -> 6.5-7.5, 10 to 20 -> 7.5-9.0, 20 to 50 -> 9.0-10.0 (cap). National average growth (~4%) maps to approximately 6.0. Computed in Python (not SQL) due to piecewise function complexity. Null if employment_change_pct is null.

grw_score_rounded.transformationDescription: Integer rounding of grw_score via _round_half_up() (floor(x + 0.5) to match DuckDB ROUND behavior, not Python banker's rounding). Produces integer 1-10 for pentagon display. Null if grw_score is null.

median_annual_wage.transformationDescription: Direct carry from Silver. Median annual wage in dollars. Null for 23 occupations. Used as input to wage_percentile_overall, wage_percentile_education_tier, and data_completeness derivations.

wage_available.transformationDescription: Direct carry from Silver. Boolean convenience flag (True if median_annual_wage is not null). Used as input to confidence_tier derivation in Gold. 809 True, 23 False.

wage_percentile_overall.transformationDescription: PERCENT_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY median_annual_wage) computed in DuckDB. Critical null-safety: computed on a filtered CTE that EXCLUDES null wages, then LEFT JOINed back to the base rows on soc_code. If nulls participate in PERCENT_RANK, DuckDB places them at ~0.185, corrupting all positions. Result is 0.0-1.0 for 809 rows with wage data, null for 23 rows without.

wage_percentile_education_tier.transformationDescription: PERCENT_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY education_code ORDER BY median_annual_wage) computed in DuckDB. Shows how well this career pays relative to peers needing the same education level. Same null-safety as wage_percentile_overall: computed on a filtered CTE excluding null wages, then LEFT JOINed back. Result is 0.0-1.0 within each education tier for rows with wage data, null for 23 rows without.

wage_tier.transformationDescription: Bucketed from wage_percentile_overall via CASE expression in DuckDB SQL. Five tiers: <0.25 -> 'low', 0.25-0.50 -> 'below_average', 0.50-0.75 -> 'above_average', 0.75-0.90 -> 'high', >=0.90 -> 'very_high'. Null if wage_percentile_overall is null (23 rows).

education_code.transformationDescription: Direct carry from Silver. BLS education level code (1=Doctoral through 8=No formal). No transformation applied in Gold.

education_level_name.transformationDescription: Direct carry from Silver. Normalized education level name derived in Silver from education_code lookup.

work_experience_code.transformationDescription: Direct carry from Silver. BLS work experience code (1=5+ years, 2=Less than 5 years, 3=None). No transformation applied in Gold.

training_code.transformationDescription: Direct carry from Silver. BLS training code (1=Internship/residency through 6=None). No transformation applied in Gold.

market_score.transformationDescription: Combined growth + opportunity signal: market_score = 0.6 * grw_score + 0.4 * openings_score. The grw_score component comes from the piecewise linear function on employment_change_pct. The openings_score component is computed as 1.0 + 9.0 * PERCENT_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY openings_annual_avg) in DuckDB, mapping the rank to a 1.0-10.0 scale. Growth direction gets 60% weight because it signals trajectory; openings volume gets 40% because it signals opportunity breadth. Computed in Python after SQL provides openings_score. Null if grw_score is null or openings_annual_avg is null.

market_score_rounded.transformationDescription: Integer rounding of market_score via _round_half_up() (floor(x + 0.5) to match DuckDB ROUND behavior). Produces integer 1-10 for display. Null if market_score is null.

confidence_tier.transformationDescription: Occupation-level data quality tier computed via CASE expression in DuckDB SQL. Priority order: (1) wage_available = FALSE -> 'low' (23 rows; can't compute ERN stat), (2) broad_occupation_flag = TRUE OR catchall_flag = TRUE -> 'medium' (heterogeneous groups; guidance less specific), (3) else -> 'high' (specific occupation with full data). Required field, never null. Expected distribution: majority 'high', ~77 'medium', 23 'low'.

data_completeness.transformationDescription: Ratio of non-null core fields among 4 specified fields (median_annual_wage, employment_current, employment_change_pct, openings_annual_avg) divided by 4.0. Computed via CASE/SUM expression in DuckDB SQL. Possible values: 0.0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0. Expected: most rows at 1.0 (all 4 fields present), 23 rows at 0.75 (missing median_annual_wage only). Required field, never null.

backs_stats.transformationDescription: Static literal string 'ERN,GRW' set for all 832 rows. Documents which FutureProof pentagon stats this occupation data product feeds. Set in Python after DuckDB SQL execution. No input fields; value is a constant defined by the spec.

backs_bosses.transformationDescription: Static literal string 'Market,Ceiling' set for all 832 rows. Documents which FutureProof boss fights this occupation data product feeds. Set in Python after DuckDB SQL execution. No input fields; value is a constant defined by the spec.

source_load_date.transformationDescription: Direct carry from Silver. Date the original BLS data entered the raw zone. Preserves provenance chain: Bronze load_date -> Silver source_load_date -> Gold source_load_date.

promoted_at.transformationDescription: UTC timestamp generated at Gold promotion time via datetime.datetime.now(tz=datetime.timezone.utc). Set in add_record_ids() after all derivations are complete. Independent of any Silver timestamp. Replaces Silver's ingested_at in the metadata lineage chain.

record_id (Silver).reason: Silver's record_id (prefix 'ooh') is replaced by a new Gold record_id (prefix 'op'). The Gold grain hash uses the same key fields (soc_code) but a different prefix, producing a different hash value.

employment_change.reason: Redundant with employment_change_pct for Gold consumers. Absolute employment change is less useful than percentage for GRW stat scoring. Available in Silver base.bls_ooh if needed for downstream analysis.

median_wage_capped.reason: All False in current data (interactive export has no top-coded wages). The wage_available boolean flag is more useful for Gold consumers. Preserved in Silver for lineage.

education_typical.reason: Redundant with education_level_name (derived from education_code in Silver). Gold carries the normalized name, drops the raw BLS label string.

work_experience.reason: Redundant with work_experience_code. The integer code is sufficient for Gold consumers and downstream joins.

training_typical.reason: Redundant with training_code. The integer code is sufficient for Gold consumers and downstream joins.

ingested_at.reason: Silver zone metadata timestamp. Replaced by promoted_at in Gold zone. The promotion timestamp is the relevant metadata for Gold consumers.
